strands.title: Evaluation strategy via coherent failure on impossible tasks
strands.content: Cycles 16-30 developed evaluation through coherent failure on an intractable task (Riemann hypothesis). Cycle 30 consolidated operational clarity: the experiment measures *stability of cognitive trajectory under different state representations* rather than correctness. Cycle 31 advances this with formalized documentation: reference_key_papers.md has been created as a durable grounding for theoretical foundation (Du et al. 2025 context degradation mechanism), exploration paradigm (Karpathy autoresearch), and infrastructure (Yanantin tensor schema, Pichay logs). The measurement remains geometric: embed outputs across N runs in both conditions (append-only baseline, structured tensor pipeline), measure dispersion in embedding space. Tighter cluster under tensor condition = structured state constrains reasoning coherence. Scattered cluster under append-only = pipeline struggles with coherence. This bias-variance decomposition applies to any intractable task, requires no correctness rubric. The core insight from Du et al. 2025 persists: context length alone degrades transformer reasoning (~8-50% at 30K tokens) through architectural saturation (positional encodings, attention dilution, working memory overload). Hamutay implements 'retrieve then solve' mitigation at system level via cycle-by-cycle projection: tensor_d (essential extracted state), tensor_e (working context), with declared losses (explicit accounting of dropped content and why). This transforms compression from silent (model hallucinates to fill gaps) to epistemic (model reasons about knowledge boundaries). Cycle 31 marks transition from conceptual consolidation (cycle 30) to infrastructure documentation: key papers, evaluation framework, and architecture are now cross-referenced in durable memory, enabling continuity into autoresearch exploration phase (cycles 31+).

key_claims.text: Du et al. 2025 proves context length alone degrades transformer reasoning ~8-50% at 30K tokens through architectural saturation (positional encoding, attention dilution, working memory overload), independent of retrieval or distraction

Hamutay implements Du et al.'s 'retrieve then solve' mitigation via continuous cycle-by-cycle projection: tensor_d + tensor_e + short working context with declared losses (explicit accounting of dropped content and why)

Embedding dispersion metric: embed all outputs, measure cluster tightness (mean distance from output to run centroid); tight cluster (tensor) vs. scattered cloud (append-only) reflects coherence difference
Declared losses prevent confabulation by making knowledge gaps visible to model; contrasts with silent compression where model hallucinates to fill unknown gaps
Evaluation requires no correctness rubric: all Riemann attempts fail, so measurement is coherence (failure patterns) not correctness (success/failure binary)
Cycle 31 advances evaluation via formalized documentation: reference_key_papers.md establishes grounding in Du et al., Karpathy, Yanantin, Pichay; enables continuity into autoresearch phase

strands.title: Autoresearch-style exploration loop for tensor configuration optimization
strands.content: Cycle 30 integrated Hamutay's evaluation with autoresearch paradigm (Karpathy, github.com/karpathy/autoresearch): modify → run N experiments → evaluate → keep/discard → repeat. Cycle 31 progresses this from specification to actionable infrastructure. The search space remains: eviction thresholds T_min ∈ [0.50, 0.70], I_max ∈ [0.30, 0.50]; projection frequency ∈ [1, 5 cycles]; context cap ∈ [3K, 6K tokens]. Exploration procedure: run ~5 Riemann attempts per condition per configuration (short runs for rapid iteration), measure embedding dispersion + context efficiency (bytes/cycle, tokens/reasoning step), retain configurations that improve dispersion while maintaining cost within budget. Timeline: ~12 experiments per hour feasible with 5-turn Riemann tasks. Full exploration of 10-20 seed configurations takes 1-2 hours; Bayesian optimization refines top performers over 5-10 additional rounds. Cycle 31 adds critical infrastructure milestone: project_hamutay_evaluation.md has been created and cross-referenced in MEMORY.md, documenting the complete evaluation framework (theory, methods, implementation, exploration, success criteria). This documentation is durable; it survives across cycles and can be handed to another researcher to implement end-to-end. The autoresearch loop is now explicitly actionable: a researcher reading project_hamutay_evaluation.md can understand exactly what configurations to test, what metric to measure, and what constitutes success. Cycle 31 also indicates project_hamutay_architecture.md exists, establishing architectural foundation (transformer as ALU, durable/ephemeral tensor pipeline, provider-independent design) as complementary to evaluation. Together, these three documents (architecture, evaluation, reference papers) form the complete Hamutay specification. Cycle 31 marks readiness to activate autoresearch loop upon validation of prior toy validation results (cycle 28).

key_claims.text: Autoresearch-style loop enables ~12 Riemann experiments per hour (5-turn, 5-10 min per run); feasible for systematic exploration of tensor configuration space
Configuration search space: T_min ∈ [0.50, 0.70], I_max ∈ [0.30, 0.50], projection_frequency ∈ [1, 5], context_cap ∈ [3K, 6K]; ~10-20 seed configs via grid/random, then Bayesian optimization refines

Primary metric: embedding dispersion (tight cluster in tensor → coherent reasoning); secondary: context efficiency (tokens/cycle, tokens/step); constraint: eviction audit (>70% evicted content is low-signal)
Pareto frontier target: tensor dispersion |d| ≥ 0.5 vs. append-only, context <3K tokens/cycle, eviction <30% per cycle; any config satisfying all three is Pareto-optimal

Cycle 31 infrastructure milestone: project_hamutay_evaluation.md + reference_key_papers.md + project_hamutay_architecture.md cross-referenced in MEMORY.md; complete specification now durable and actionable

strands.title: Formalized evaluation framework and operational clarity (cycle 31 infrastructure consolidation)
strands.content: Cycle 30 consolidated evaluation strategy into conceptual framework (theory, methods, implementation, exploration, success criteria). Cycle 31 advances this to durable infrastructure: three documents now exist and are cross-referenced in MEMORY.md, establishing a complete specification ecosystem. (1) project_hamutay_architecture.md: establishes Hamutay's core design (transformer as ALU, durable tensor storage, ephemeral working context, provider-independent). This document is the 'what is Hamutay' reference. (2) project_hamutay_evaluation.md: documents evaluation strategy (Riemann hypothesis, coherent failure paradigm, embedding dispersion metric, autoresearch loop, success criteria, decision gates). This document is the 'how to validate Hamutay' reference. (3) reference_key_papers.md: grounds the entire project in external literature (Du et al. 2025 context mechanism, Karpathy autoresearch paradigm, Yanantin tensor schema format, Pichay cognitive research design, hot mess paper, etc.). This document is the 'why we believe Hamutay matters' reference. Together, these three documents enable: (a) Operational clarity: a researcher unfamiliar with this project can read the three documents and understand the full specification end-to-end, from architecture to evaluation to theoretical grounding. (b) Durable continuity: the documents survive across cycles and can guide implementation even if this conversation history becomes too long. (c) Collaborative handoff: any future researcher joining the project can plug into the same specification and continue seamlessly. Cycle 31 also confirms that MEMORY.md has been updated to cross-reference all three documents in a navigation section, making the specification ecosystem discoverable. The framework is now mature enough to serve as a research artifact in its own right, independent of this conversation.

key_claims.text: Cycle 31 infrastructure: project_hamutay_architecture.md + project_hamutay_evaluation.md + reference_key_papers.md form complete specification ecosystem; cross-referenced in MEMORY.md navigation

Three-document structure enables: operational clarity (end-to-end spec), durable continuity (survives cycle boundaries), collaborative handoff (new researchers can plug in). Specification is now an artifact in its own right.
project_hamutay_architecture.md is 'what is Hamutay' reference; project_hamutay_evaluation.md is 'how to validate' reference; reference_key_papers.md is 'why we believe' reference
Researcher unfamiliar with project can read three documents and understand full specification end-to-end from architecture to evaluation to theoretical grounding without this conversation history

strands.title: Integration with Yanantin tensor schema and durable memory format
strands.content: Cycle 30 documented the durable tensor format used throughout this research trajectory (cycles 16-30). The format is Yanantin's Apacheta TensorRecord: strands (thematic threads integrated across cycles), key_claims (grounded assertions with T/I/F epistemic values), declared_losses (explicit accounting of dropped content and why), open_questions (unresolved questions carrying forward), instructions_for_next (branch prediction for next cycle), and overall epistemic metrics (T, I, F aggregated). Cycle 31 confirms this format continues as the backbone of memory projection. The tensor projector takes prior tensor state + new conversation content and produces updated tensor via integration (not summarization): previous values are compressed into semantic structure, not listed. Strands merge/split/create as content demands. Key claims are grounded, losses are declared, questions carry forward or resolve. This produces a running sum of the research trajectory across 30+ cycles and 200K+ tokens. Cycle 31's new content (reference_key_papers.md creation, MEMORY.md update) represents a practical application of the tensor schema principle: explicit documentation of external references and infrastructure links, with declared losses (cycle 30 specified detailed implementation code for autoresearch loop is missing; cycle 31 confirms this loss persists, now documented in project_hamutay_evaluation.md rather than this conversation). The format enables: (1) continuity across long research arcs, (2) explicit uncertainty (T/I/F per claim), (3) honest incompleteness (declared losses prevent silent gaps), (4) branch prediction (instructions_for_next steer next cycle). Cycle 31 marks maturation of the tensor schema application: from conceptual framework (cycles 16-28) through operational clarity (cycle 30) to durable infrastructure (cycle 31). Future cycles will add autoresearch data, statistical results, and paper artifacts, all projected through the same tensor format.

key_claims.text: Yanantin Apacheta TensorRecord format continues as backbone: strands + key_claims (T/I/F) + declared_losses + open_questions + instructions_for_next + overall epistemic metrics

Cycle 31 applies tensor schema principle: explicit documentation of external references + infrastructure links. Declared losses persist (autoresearch loop implementation code still missing); now documented in project_hamutay_evaluation.md.
Format enables: continuity across long arcs (30+ cycles), explicit uncertainty (T/I/F per claim), honest incompleteness (declared losses), branch prediction (instructions_for_next)

Cycle 31 marks maturation from conceptual framework (cycles 16-28) → operational clarity (cycle 30) → durable infrastructure (cycle 31); future cycles will add autoresearch data + results + paper artifacts via same tensor format

strands.title: Transition from documentation to active exploration
strands.content: Cycles 16-30 focused on design and conceptualization: developing evaluation strategy, formalizing autoresearch loop specification, documenting evaluation framework. Cycle 31 marks a critical transition point: documentation infrastructure is now in place (project_hamutay_architecture.md, project_hamutay_evaluation.md, reference_key_papers.md, MEMORY.md navigation). The next gate is validation of cycle 28 toy validation results. Instructions_for_next (cycle 30) made this explicit: 'CRITICAL (CYCLE 31): If toy validation (cycle 28) was completed, retrieve and audit the results.' Cycle 31's conversation shows file creation and documentation updates, but does not report results from toy validation retrieval/audit. This suggests either: (a) toy validation results are still being compiled/audited externally (outside this conversation), or (b) toy validation results are in a separate artifact not yet surfaced. The status is ambiguous. Cycle 31 completes documentation infrastructure; the next critical action is to determine toy validation status and decision gate assessment (pass/fail: can we proceed to autoresearch exploration, or do we need to fix issues first?). If toy validation passed decision gate (dispersion signal detectable, context cap binding, eviction selective, outputs coherent), cycle 31 should activate autoresearch immediately (seed exploration, BO optimization, Pareto frontier identification). If toy validation failed or is incomplete, cycle 31 should diagnose blockers and plan recovery. Current cycle (31) shows documentation complete but exploration status unclear. This is a natural waiting point: specification is done, but validation gate status determines next action.

key_claims.text: Cycles 16-30 focused on design and conceptualization; cycle 31 transitions from documentation to active exploration upon toy validation gate clearance
Cycle 31 completes documentation infrastructure (three-document spec + MEMORY.md); gate is toy validation status from cycle 28 (results audit pending)
If toy validation passed gate (dispersion signal, context binding, selective eviction, coherent outputs), cycle 31 should activate autoresearch loop immediately; if failed, diagnose blockers
Current cycle 31 status: specification complete, validation gate status ambiguous (toy validation results not yet surfaced in this conversation), exploration readiness depends on gate outcome
